rationale: The "satisfying pour into labeled jar" ASMR-style 3D render aligns well with Marble Sort!'s calm/satisfying mood and skews to the same 25-55 female casual audience, so the audience overlap is the strongest asset. However, the mechanical translation is weak: pouring spice into a single jar doesn't visually telegraph color-sorting, conveyor timing, or tap-to-release physics — the player won't understand the core loop from this hook. Recommend adapting the archetype by swapping the pepper-in-jar for multi-colored marbles pouring/sorting into matching colored jars on a conveyor, and shifting the palette from the muted pink/sage to the game's lavender + orange accent to preserve brand recognition.
